topic: Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry
change_summary: EXP-298 defense neutralization integrated by decider in commit bebbb00 (2026-05-10T07:19Z): detail_evidence rewritten 1365 chars -> 4778 chars (+250%). Adds explicit kernel acknowledgement, longitudinal arc calc (|152-180|=|208-180|=28 deg), unfalsifiability pivot (no published tolerance), dome-internal threshold cites (WIN-029 5.3%, WIN-027 R^2=0.7874, WIN-030 48.6% RMS), v22 cross-equator failure cite, four anticipated-objection paragraphs in EXP file. detail_verdict_text unchanged at 1196 chars (post-hoc framing untouched per EXP notes). tldr unchanged.
our_argument_summary: Verdict (Refuted by Data) holds. EXP-298 substantially closes the c4/c5 hole on the 180-degree framing by adopting an explicit dual-reading frame (exact-180 quantitative vs vague-antipodal unfalsifiable) and acknowledging 180 as our charitable steelman rather than dome-attributed wording. ISS-1910 (unanchored '15.6% exceeds dome thresholds') and ISS-1911 (stale '~80W' agonic position) both look closeable -- the new text names WIN-029 5.3% as the comparator and updates the western agonic to '95-100W per IGRF-13/WMM 2025' -- the decider may have missed closing them. However the rewrite introduces a new strawman risk with the same shape as the original: it asserts 'The dome's confirming text invokes the agonic lines (longitudes where magnetic declination = 0)' even though the dome's published WIN-028 text in raw-text/02-wins.txt:113 contains zero mention of agonic lines or magnetic declination -- the dome's WIN-028 is one line, no mechanism. The mechanism-paragraph is therefore vulnerable to the same defender attack the EXP successfully addressed for the 180 framing: 'I never invoked agonic lines; you invented the mechanism and refuted your own assumption.' Plus four moderate framing issues: (1) 15.6% denominator choice (28/180) is unjustified -- on a circle, expected random separation from antipodal is 90 degrees, so 28 is closer to antipodal than random by 3x; defender will say we picked the denominator that maximized apparent error; (2) WIN-029, WIN-027, WIN-030 are cited as authoritative tolerance benchmarks without acknowledging our own verdicts on those WINs (Self-Contradicted, Misleading, Misleading respectively) -- needs explicit 'we don't endorse this benchmark, we use it for internal-consistency attack' framing; (3) R^2 = 0.7874 and 48.6% RMS reduction conflated with 5.3% percentage tolerance -- three different statistical measures presented as if comparable thresholds; (4) v22 cross-equator failure cited as comparable failure threshold, but per raw-text/06-evolution.txt:9,56 the v22 failure was 'Southern arc distances 2x too long' = 100% deviation, which actually establishes a much LARGER failure threshold than 15.6% -- the cite is a self-own. None changes the verdict. All five are minor_edit class.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt:113 (one-line title only -- 'Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED')
raw-text/06-evolution.txt:54-57 (v22 ADDRESSED DIRECTLY -- 'Southern arc distances 2x too long -- FAILS')
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c5. WIN-028 has no monitor script, no validation logic, no data-fetch line, no hardcoded numerical target. monitor.py absent from raw-text/ at this snapshot AND absent from the entire repo (find -name monitor.py returns empty). The dome's WIN-028 is published as a single title line with no derivation, no formula, no mechanism statement.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Pure static folklore assertion. No agonic-line position calculation, no insurance-rate retrieval, no geomagnetic comparison, no anomaly-rate ingestion, no longitude-separation computation in dome code. Unchanged from c5.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None -- no validation performed by dome.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: monitoring='none' tag in data/wins.json is correct. No regression.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Re-verified at c6 post-EXP-298: grep of raw-text/ for 'bermuda', 'devil', 'agonic', 'symmetry' returns only the WIN-028 title line in raw-text/02-wins.txt:113 and the v50.4 entry in raw-text/06-evolution.txt:44. monitor.py absent from raw-text/ AND from anywhere in the repo (find . -name monitor.py 2>/dev/null returns empty). No API call, no data-fetch logic, no comparison logic for this WIN. Tag remains 'none'.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Bermuda Triangle / Devil's Sea 'disappearance zone' framing is paranormal folklore, not standard physics. Agonic lines are real geophysics but the dome's WIN-028 text doesn't actually invoke them (see hole #1). No standard-physics phenomenon is being relabeled here. Unchanged from c5.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Bermuda Triangle legend: Gaddis 1964 (Argosy magazine), Berlitz 1974 (book). Devil's Sea legend: Berlitz 1974. Agonic line positions (if invoked): mapped since Halley 1701. Dome v50.4 added WIN-028 on 2026-03-09 per raw-text/06-evolution.txt:44. Pure retrodiction of pre-existing folklore. detail_verdict_text post-hoc paragraph is unchanged from c5 and remains correct. Unchanged from c5.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula on the dome site connects disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, sun_altitude=5733 km, or moon_altitude=2534 km to the longitudes of Bermuda or Devil's Sea. Re-verified by grep across raw-text/ at c6 -- no derivation, no parameter dependence, no quantitative tolerance. Unchanged from c5.

holes_found.description: STRAWMAN RISK -- new in c6, same class as c4/c5 180-degree finding. The EXP-298 rewrite states 'The mechanism is also wrong. The dome's confirming text invokes the agonic lines (longitudes where magnetic declination = 0).' But the dome's published WIN-028 in raw-text/02-wins.txt:113 contains exactly one line: 'Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED' -- no mention of agonic lines, no mention of magnetic declination, no mechanism statement at all. grep across raw-text/ for 'agonic' returns zero hits anywhere in dome material. The agonic-line attribution appears to be a curmudgeon-side characterization (likely seeded in WIN-028.json cycle-1 review when c1 wrote 'the dome claims TWO parts: ... 180-degree longitudinal symmetry related to agonic lines') that has propagated through every cycle and now sits in our published page as an attributed dome claim. This is the SAME error pattern the c4/c5 reviews already caught for the 180-degree framing -- attributing a specific mechanism to the dome that the dome's own published material doesn't contain. The whole 'mechanism is also wrong' paragraph (~1/3 of the EXP-298 expanded text) collapses if a defender simply quotes the dome's actual one-line WIN-028 entry and asks 'where did I invoke agonic lines?'
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the SAME dual-reading frame the EXP successfully applied to 180 degrees: 'The dome publishes no mechanism for the claimed symmetry. We test the only standard physical mechanism that could plausibly link two folklore zones at the same latitude across hemispheres -- agonic-line correlation -- as our charitable steelman. Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025, the western agonic ... [unchanged geographic content]. If the dome wishes to propose a different mechanism, the burden is to publish one; without a published mechanism, the WIN reduces to 'two folklore zones happen to sit ~152 degrees apart,' which is the unfalsifiable reading addressed above.' This addresses the strawman charge without losing the IGRF/WMM 2025 quantitative content. Decider can self-apply as a moderate text patch (changing ~80 words of the mechanism paragraph).

holes_found.description: DENOMINATOR-CHOICE FRAMING -- new in c6. The text says 'The 28-degree gap is a 15.6% deviation' (28/180 = 0.155). The denominator choice is unjustified. On a circle, two random points have expected separation from antipodal of 90 degrees (uniform distribution), so 28 degrees is closer to antipodal than random by ~3x. A defender will say: 'Your 15.6% is computed by dividing the deviation by half the circle. Why not by the full circle (28/360 = 7.8%)? Or by random-baseline (28/90 = 31% from-random)? You picked the denominator that maximized the apparent error.' The cleanest defense is to anchor the choice: 'The dome's claim is symmetry around a 180-degree antipodal target, so the natural metric is fraction of the target value -- a quantitative model claiming X but measuring 0.844X is off by 15.6%, which is the standard form for relative error.' One sentence inserted before the WIN-029 cite would close this.
holes_found.recommendation: Insert one sentence justifying the 15.6% denominator: 'Because the dome's claim is symmetry around a target value of 180 degrees (the only standard mathematical referent for geometric symmetry between two surface points), the natural relative-error metric is deviation as fraction of the target -- 28/180 = 15.6%.' This pre-empts the denominator-cherry-picking attack.

holes_found.description: INTERNAL-CONSISTENCY FRAMING MISSING -- new in c6, but explicitly anticipated by c5. The new text cites WIN-029 5.3%, WIN-027 R^2=0.7874, WIN-030 48.6% RMS reduction as 'the dome's own confirmation thresholds.' Our review verdicts these WINs Self-Contradicted (029), Misleading (027), and Misleading (030). c5 explicitly recommended 'frame as internal-consistency attack, not endorsement of WIN-029's 5.3%' -- but the EXP-298 rewrite doesn't include that framing. As written, a defender could argue: 'You yourselves don't accept these benchmarks elsewhere on your site; you can't simultaneously call them invalid AND use them as authoritative tolerance comparisons. Either the dome's threshold pattern is meaningful (in which case WIN-029 isn't Self-Contradicted) or it isn't (in which case 5.3% has no bearing on this WIN).' The fix is one half-sentence acknowledging the move is internal-consistency.
holes_found.recommendation: Insert one parenthetical clause in the WIN-029 cite: 'WIN-029 takes a 5.3% agreement... as confirmatory (we do not endorse 5.3% as a meaningful tolerance -- WIN-029 is itself verdicted Self-Contradicted in our review -- but cite it here to show the dome's own framework treats single-digit residuals as the bar).' Same lighter touch can be inserted near the WIN-027 R^2 and WIN-030 RMS cites or rolled into one parenthetical.

holes_found.description: METRIC-TYPE CONFLATION -- new in c6. The text presents 5.3% (WIN-029, residual percentage), R^2=0.7874 (WIN-027, goodness-of-fit), and 48.6% RMS reduction (WIN-030, fit-improvement-over-prior-model) as a single category of 'internal confirmation thresholds.' These are three distinct statistical measures. R^2 is not an upper bound on residual percentage -- a model with R^2=0.79 can have arbitrarily large residuals if the data range is large. RMS reduction is a relative-improvement metric -- 48.6% RMS reduction means 'this fit is 48.6% better than the baseline' but tells you nothing about the absolute residual size. Citing all three in a single 'thresholds' list invites the defender to say: 'You are conflating three different statistical concepts to pad the case that single-digit numbers are the dome's confirmation bar. R^2 = 0.79 doesn't bound residual percentage, and a 48.6% RMS reduction doesn't either.'
holes_found.recommendation: Either drop the R^2 and RMS cites (keeping only the WIN-029 5.3% as the like-for-like comparator -- it IS a residual percentage, comparable to our 15.6% residual percentage), OR keep all three but re-frame: 'The dome's published quantitative confirmations across V51.1 use single-digit residuals (WIN-029 5.3%) and high-fit statistics (WIN-027 R^2 0.79, WIN-030 48.6% RMS reduction over baseline) as the bar. A 15.6% residual on the symmetry target is roughly 3x the WIN-029 residual benchmark and would be unaccounted-for variance in a fit context.' The first option (drop R^2 and RMS) is cleaner and removes the conflation attack vector entirely.

holes_found.description: v22 SELF-OWN -- new in c6. The text invokes 'v22 found circular disc fails on southern/cross-equator distances' as evidence of the dome's own retrospective failure threshold, suggesting 15.6% is on that order. But per raw-text/06-evolution.txt:9 the actual failure quote is 'v22 found circular disc fails on southern distances' and per raw-text/06-evolution.txt:56 the specific numeric was 'Southern arc distances 2x too long -- FAILS.' That's 100% deviation, not 15%. The dome's own historical failure threshold from v22 is therefore much LARGER than 15.6%, not comparable to it. As written, the cite actually undermines our argument -- a defender will say: 'Your v22 example proves my point: the dome's historical failure threshold per its own evolution log is 100% deviation. 15.6% is well within tolerance by that exact benchmark.' The cite needs to either be dropped, or reframed honestly: 'v22 only flagged 100% deviation as failure; in the absence of any sub-100% threshold elsewhere, the dome's framework gives no published rule for which sub-100% deviations count as confirmation -- which is precisely the unfalsifiability the vague-reading paragraph addressed.'
holes_found.recommendation: Drop the v22 cite from the EXP-298 paragraph -- it is not in fact a comparable failure threshold to 15.6%. The internal-consistency case rests adequately on WIN-029 5.3% alone. If the v22 cite is retained, add the explicit '100% deviation, not 15%' caveat and reframe as 'no published sub-100% threshold exists' -- which strengthens the unfalsifiability argument rather than competing with it.

holes_found.description: DEVIL'S SEA LATITUDE CHERRY-PICK -- new in c6. The text gives Devil's Sea coordinates as '~25 deg N, 137 deg E.' The Devil's Sea / Dragon's Triangle in folklore is variously described as spanning 25-35 deg N (Berlitz 1974 places it south of Honshu extending toward Iwo Jima and Guam), with the typical centroid around 30 deg N, not 25 deg N. Picking 25 deg N matches the Bermuda Triangle's 25 deg N latitude and frames as longitudinal-symmetry-at-equal-latitude, but at the more typical Devil's Sea centroid (~30 deg N), the latitudes don't match either. A defender can say: 'You picked the southern edge of the Devil's Sea region to manufacture a same-latitude framing; at the actual centroid the latitudes differ by ~5 degrees.' Doesn't change the verdict but a careful reader will notice.
holes_found.recommendation: Either anchor the 25 deg N choice ('we use 25 deg N as a central estimate; folklore variants span 25-35 deg N') or drop the latitude assertion and discuss longitude separation only ('two folklore zones at mid-northern latitudes, separated by ~152 deg in longitude'). The longitude-only framing is cleaner and side-steps the latitude-cherry-pick objection without weakening any argument.

holes_found.description: UNSOURCED 50-DEGREE THRESHOLD -- new in c6. The vague-reading paragraph says 'any pair of points within roughly 50 degrees of antipodal could pass.' The 50 degrees is unsourced. Where does it come from? If we'd said 30 degrees, the dome's 28-degree separation would pass and our argument would fail. The 50 degrees appears reverse-engineered to make the argument work. A defender will pick on this: 'Your 50 is no more sourced than my hypothetical tolerance. Two arbitrary numbers don't establish unfalsifiability.'
holes_found.recommendation: Either anchor 50 deg with a citation or argument ('half-circle gives 90 deg, antipodal gives 0; the midpoint of folklore-zone-separation distributions across all named regions is roughly 50 deg' -- if true, source it; if not true, drop the number) or replace with 'any deviation up to ~150 degrees could pass under a sufficiently lax reading -- the dome's published lack-of-tolerance is itself the unfalsifiability, not the specific tolerance value.' The second framing is cleaner and doesn't introduce a number to defend.

holes_found.description: WMM 2025 NOT CITATION-ANCHORED -- carry-forward concern from c5 citations_unchecked. The EXP-298 text invokes 'Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025' for the western agonic at 95-100 deg W and the eastern agonic at 100-120 deg E, but provides no DOI or URL. WMM 2025 is real (released December 2024 by NOAA/NCEI/British Geological Survey covering 2025-2030) and the cited declination positions are correct, but the citation should be anchored. ISS-1911 was opened on the older '~80W' framing and is technically closeable now (the position has been updated), but the new WMM 2025 cite has no inline URL.
holes_found.recommendation: Add inline URL: 'Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025...'. Optional: also note that ISS-1911 should be closed in the next decider self-apply cycle since the position-update fix has landed.

stronger_arguments.description: The unfalsifiability argument in the new vague-reading paragraph is the strongest single move and could be promoted from a sub-bullet to a structural pillar. The dome lists 'falsified' as a possible state in its own ledger; a WIN with no published tolerance and no published mechanism cannot transition to 'falsified' under any conceivable observation, which contradicts the dome's own evidentiary framework. This argument is robust to all defender counters -- the defender either publishes a tolerance (and accepts our quantitative reading) or doesn't (and accepts the unfalsifiability). The current text buries this in a sub-bullet; promoting it would tighten the whole detail_evidence around the strongest available attack.
sources: raw-text/02-wins.txt header (the dome's framework lists 'falsified' as a state)

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-WIN consistency case: the same 'no published tolerance' pattern affects multiple geometric-symmetry WINs in V51.1. If the curmudgeon does a cross-WIN survey of which WINs publish quantitative tolerances vs which are bare-assertion confirmations, the pattern likely shows that bare-assertion WINs cluster in the post-hoc retrodiction cohort while published-tolerance WINs cluster in the prospective-prediction cohort -- which would itself be a structural argument that the dome's confirmation framework is post-hoc by construction, not by accident. WIN-028 is one data point in that pattern. Future holistic check candidate.
sources: raw-text/02-wins.txt (PROS-* prospective WINs vs WIN-NNN retrodictive WINs structure)

kernel_of_truth.description: The EXP-298 rewrite gets the kernel right: the Bermuda Triangle (centroid ~25 deg N, ~71 deg W -- see data/wins.json:028 detail_evidence kernel paragraph) and the Devil's Sea (~25 deg N region, ~137 deg E per the same paragraph) really are near-opposite in longitude, with short-arc separation 152 deg and long-arc 208 deg. The arithmetic |152-180| = |208-180| = 28 deg is correct (verified: 360 - 152 = 208; |152 - 180| = 28; |208 - 180| = 28; 28/180 = 15.55%). 'Notable longitudinal coincidence' between two named folklore zones is a real, observable, geographic regularity. The EXP rewrite's explicit acknowledgment of this kernel before pivoting to refutation is a substantive improvement over the c4/c5 framing.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The longitudinal coincidence is geographic, not derivational -- the dome doesn't show a path from disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km (data/wins.json: code_analysis derives_from_dome=false) to the specific longitudes of Bermuda or Devil's Sea. Without derivation, the kernel is observation, not prediction. Plus the disappearance phenomenon itself is refuted three independent ways per the new detail_evidence final paragraph (NOAA at https://oceanservice.noaa.gov/facts/bermudatri.html, Lloyd's of London insurance pricing, USCG 1975 review), and the post-hoc carve-out at #posthoc-standard requires the confirming material to be 'not plausibly known to the author at write-time' -- which fails for centuries-old cartographic coordinates and mid-20th-century magazine articles per detail_verdict_text. Verdict (Refuted by Data) holds even after granting the geographic kernel.

deeper_analysis:
WIN-028.c6 reviews EXP-298 (defense neutralization) integrated by decider commit bebbb00 on 2026-05-10T07:19Z, queued at queue_id 325 / pushed_at 2026-05-10T07:16:21.405Z, class=deep-attack (singleton per Step 8a gate 1). Fresh clone + git pull --rebase confirms current data/wins.json[id=028] has detail_evidence_length=4778 (vs c5's 1365), all other fingerprint fields unchanged. Reviewed against the EXP-298 file (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-298.json) and against the dome's source material (raw-text/02-wins.txt:113, raw-text/06-evolution.txt).

Main finding: EXP-298 is a substantive improvement on the 180-degree framing (the c4 hole #1 / c5 hole #1 carry-over), adopting the dual-reading frame the c5 review explicitly recommended -- 'we do not attribute the literal number 180 to the dome; we use it because it is the only standard mathematical referent.' Plus the unfalsifiability pivot, the explicit arc calc (|152-180|=|208-180|=28), the dome-internal threshold cites, and the four anticipated objections in the EXP file -- all are credible adversarial-defensive moves. The c5 verdict (recommended_action=minor_edit, three residual minor holes, current_verdict_holds=true) is broadly vindicated by the rewrite. ISS-1910 (unanchored '15.6% exceeds dome thresholds') and ISS-1911 (stale '~80W' agonic position) both look closeable now -- the new text addresses both -- the decider may have missed closing them when integrating EXP-298. Surfaced in summary_for_decider for next BAU cycle.

New finding (this c6's main contribution): the EXP-298 rewrite repeats the SAME class of attribution-without-source error the c4/c5 reviews caught for the 180-degree framing, but for the agonic-line mechanism. The new text states 'The mechanism is also wrong. The dome's confirming text invokes the agonic lines (longitudes where magnetic declination = 0).' I verified by grep across all dome raw-text that 'agonic' returns ZERO hits, and 'declination' returns one hit unrelated to WIN-028 (WIN-020 lunar declination). The dome's WIN-028 is one line: 'Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED' -- no mechanism. The agonic-line attribution appears to be a curmudgeon-side characterization that has propagated since cycle 1 (WIN-028.json original review explicitly wrote 'the dome claims TWO parts: ... 180-degree longitudinal symmetry related to agonic lines'). The fix is straightforward: apply the same dual-reading move to the mechanism paragraph -- 'the dome publishes no mechanism; we test the only standard physical mechanism that could plausibly link two folklore zones at the same latitude across hemispheres, agonic-line correlation, as our charitable steelman; even granting that, IGRF/WMM 2025 shows...'. This preserves the IGRF/WMM quantitative content while closing the strawman attack vector. Severity: major. Recommended_action: minor_edit (one paragraph rewrite ~80 words).

Four additional moderate framing issues (all new in c6, none verdict-changing): (a) 15.6% denominator choice is unjustified -- on a circle, expected random separation from antipodal is 90 deg, so the 28-deg gap is ~3x closer than random; the 180-deg denominator was picked without explanation and a defender will flag it. (b) WIN-029, WIN-027, WIN-030 cited as authoritative tolerance benchmarks without acknowledging our own verdicts on those WINs (Self-Contradicted, Misleading, Misleading) -- c5 explicitly anticipated this and recommended 'frame as internal-consistency attack, not endorsement' but the EXP didn't include that framing. (c) R^2 = 0.7874 and 48.6% RMS reduction conflated with 5.3% percentage tolerance -- three different statistical measures presented as comparable thresholds; defender attack vector. (d) v22 cited as 'failure threshold' but the actual v22 failure size per raw-text/06-evolution.txt:56 was '2x too long' = 100% deviation, not 15%. The cite is a self-own -- it actually establishes a much LARGER failure threshold than 15.6% and a defender will use it against us.

Three minor framing issues: (e) Devil's Sea coordinates picked at 25 deg N which is the southern edge of the folklore region (typical centroid ~30 deg N), arguably cherry-picked to match Bermuda Triangle's 25 deg N for a same-latitude framing. (f) The '50 degrees' tolerance in the vague-reading paragraph is unsourced and reverse-engineered -- if we'd said 30, the dome's 28 would pass. (g) WMM 2025 not anchored with an inline URL.

Cross-WIN check: WIN-029 (Self-Contradicted), WIN-027 (Misleading), WIN-030 (Misleading) verdicts are unchanged from c5; the cross-references in the new EXP-298 text point to WINs that are themselves not endorsed by our review, which is the basis of the moderate (b) issue above.

Advocate-mode rejoinder check (per operator preference for considering the other side): the strongest dome rejoinder to c6 is 'I never invoked agonic lines, just like I never invoked 180 degrees. Your review keeps inventing mechanisms for me to refute. Two consecutive cycles flagged the 180 issue and you fixed it; now you need to fix the same error for the agonic claim. The disappearance refutation (NOAA / Lloyd's / USCG) and the post-hoc temporal argument are both fair attacks I cannot easily counter; the symmetry-and-mechanism arguments are charitable-steelman moves you should explicitly label as such. Once you do, the WIN's verdict is unchanged but my honest defense at the structural level is that the dome's published WIN-028 is so thin -- one line -- that the only fair attack is the post-hoc one.' This rejoinder survives at rating 2 (vulnerable to fix but real until fix lands). The fix is the recommended dual-reading-frame patch on the mechanism paragraph; once applied, defense rating drops to 1. Verdict (Refuted by Data) holds throughout.

Workflow note for decider: ISS-1910 and ISS-1911 should be closed at next BAU pass -- the new EXP-298 text addresses both. ISS-1919 (defense neutralization) closure looks correct. No new ISS needed for the c6 holes IF the decider self-applies the recommended dual-reading-frame patch on the mechanism paragraph plus the four moderate framing tightenings; if not self-applied, open a single ISS covering hole #1 (the agonic strawman) and a separate consolidated ISS for holes #2-#5 (the four moderate framing issues).

At-rest verdict: Refuted by Data is correct, post-hoc tag is correct, all derives_from_dome / monitoring / relabels_standard tags are correct, current_verdict_holds=true, recommended_action=minor_edit.

citations_verified: WIN-028 raw-text content in raw-text/02-wins.txt:113 -- one-line title, no mechanism, re-verified at c6
WIN-029 5.3% match cited in raw-text/02-wins.txt:114 -- re-verified at c6
v22 failure narrative in raw-text/06-evolution.txt:9 ('fails on southern distances') and 56 ('Southern arc distances 2x too long') -- re-verified at c6
v50.4 (2026-03-09) addition in raw-text/06-evolution.txt:44 -- re-verified at c6
Halley 1701 first systematic geomagnetic chart -- historical fact
Gaddis 1964 (Argosy magazine) and Berlitz 1974 (The Bermuda Triangle book) -- popular folklore origins, public record
NOAA Bermuda Triangle page link in current detail_evidence -- present verbatim
Bermuda Triangle centroid coordinates (~25 deg N, ~71 deg W) -- average of Miami (25.7N, 80.2W), San Juan (18.5N, 66.1W), Bermuda (32.3N, 64.8W) gives ~25.5N, 70.4W -- consistent with claim
citations_failed: 'The dome's confirming text invokes the agonic lines' -- cannot be sourced. grep across all raw-text/ for 'agonic' returns zero hits. The dome's WIN-028 is one line with no mechanism statement. This is hole #1 (major).
citations_unchecked: WMM 2025 specific declination grid for the western agonic at 95-100 deg W and eastern agonic at 100-120 deg E -- the values are correct per public WMM 2025 maps, but no inline URL anchors them in the page. Carry-forward from c5; partial fix landed (~80W -> 95-100W) but URL still missing. Hole #8 (minor).
'any pair of points within roughly 50 degrees of antipodal could pass' -- the 50 deg figure is unsourced. Hole #7 (minor).

advocate_mode.best_defense: I am the dome author. The funwithscience review just rewrote my WIN-028 entry from 1365 to 4778 characters. The expansion is impressive but they've made the same error twice now in the same review. Two cycles ago they attributed '180 degrees as predicted by dome geometry' to me; I never said 180. They fixed that this cycle by explicitly labeling the 180 as their own charitable steelman -- good. But in the same rewrite they assert 'The dome's confirming text invokes the agonic lines (longitudes where magnetic declination = 0).' I never said that either. My published WIN-028 is one line: 'Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED.' Anyone can verify this on my site. They invented the agonic mechanism, refuted it with WMM 2025 data, and presented the refutation as if it bore on my actual claim. That's a strawman -- the same one their own previous reviewers caught for the 180 attribution. Beyond the strawman: their 15.6% number is computed by dividing the deviation by 180 degrees, which they don't justify -- if they'd divided by 360 they'd get 7.8%; if they'd compared to random-baseline-90 they'd get 31% from random. They cite my WIN-029 5.3% as a confirmation threshold and my WIN-027 R^2=0.7874 and my WIN-030 48.6% RMS reduction in the same breath, as if these were comparable measures -- they are three different statistics. They cite v22 cross-equator failures as evidence of my own failure threshold, but v22's actual failure size was 100% deviation per my evolution log -- which proves the opposite of what they want. The disappearance critique (NOAA / Lloyd's / USCG) and the post-hoc temporal critique I cannot easily counter -- those are honest. The symmetry and mechanism critiques are over-engineered and self-undermining.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Apply the dual-reading-frame patch to the mechanism paragraph (hole #1 -- the agonic strawman) following the same pattern the c5 review used for the 180-degree framing: 'The dome publishes no mechanism; we test the only standard physical mechanism that could plausibly link two folklore zones at the same latitude across hemispheres -- agonic-line correlation -- as our charitable steelman.' This single ~80-word rewrite closes the strawman attack vector while preserving all the IGRF/WMM 2025 quantitative content. Apply the four moderate framing tightenings (denominator anchor, internal-consistency framing, drop-or-reframe R^2/RMS conflation, drop the v22 cite or honestly reframe it as 'the dome's only retrospective failure threshold is 100%, which is itself the unfalsifiability problem'). After these patches, defense rating drops to 1 and the WIN's adversarial robustness is comparable to the strongest WINs in our review.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-027 (Misleading), WIN-029 (Self-Contradicted), WIN-030 (Misleading) verdicts unchanged from c5 -- the EXP-298 cross-references to these WINs as 'dome's own thresholds' are factually correct (the dome does cite those numbers as confirmation in its own text) but require explicit internal-consistency framing per c5's recommendation, which the EXP-298 rewrite did not include (hole #3). No new cross-WIN inconsistencies introduced by the rewrite. WIN-064 (Toroidal Ovoid SAA asymmetry) is the closest cross-WIN to WIN-028's geographic-symmetry framing but uses entirely different parameters and is not affected.

claims_checked: Bermuda Triangle centroid coordinates (25N, 71W) vs vertex average of Miami/San Juan/Bermuda
Devil's Sea coordinates (25N, 137E) vs typical folklore centroid
raw-text/02-wins.txt grep for 'declination' (one hit -- WIN-020 lunar, unrelated)
find . -name monitor.py 2>/dev/null (zero hits across entire repo)

quantitative_verification.computations: Arc arithmetic verified: 360 - 152 = 208 (correct); |152 - 180| = 28 (correct); |208 - 180| = 28 (correct); 28/180 = 0.15555... rounds to 15.6% (correct, matches text). Bermuda Triangle vertex mean: (25.7N, 80.2W), (18.5N, 66.1W), (32.3N, 64.8W) -> centroid (25.5N, 70.4W) -> claim of (25N, 71W) is consistent. Devil's Sea typical folklore centroid is closer to 30N (Berlitz 1974 places it south of Honshu/Tokyo extending to Iwo Jima/Guam) -- the (25N, 137E) coordinate is the southern edge, not the centroid (hole #6, minor). agonic / declination grep returns zero/one hit respectively, confirming the attribution-without-source for the mechanism claim (hole #1, major). monitor.py absent from entire repo -- monitoring='none' tag is correct.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Two: (1) Devil's Sea (25N, 137E) is southern-edge not centroid; (2) the v22 cite implies a 15%-class failure threshold but the actual v22 failure was 100%.

summary_for_decider: EXP-298 is a substantive improvement -- adopts the dual-reading frame for 180 deg, the unfalsifiability pivot, explicit arc arithmetic, dome-threshold anchoring, and pre-empted four anticipated objections. ISS-1910 and ISS-1911 should be closed at next BAU pass (the new text addresses both: WIN-029 5.3% is now named explicitly; western agonic updated 80W -> 95-100W per WMM 2025). One major hole (agonic strawman -- same class of error as the original 180 strawman, fixable with the same dual-reading-frame patch on the mechanism paragraph), four moderate framing issues (denominator anchor, internal-consistency framing, R^2/RMS conflation, v22 self-own), three minor framing issues (Devil's Sea latitude cherry-pick, unsourced 50 deg vague-tolerance, missing WMM 2025 inline URL). Verdict (Refuted by Data) holds; recommended_action=minor_edit. Suggest decider self-apply the major hole #1 fix as a single moderate text patch (estimated ~80 word rewrite of the 'mechanism is also wrong' paragraph) and a consolidated patch covering holes #2-#5 (estimated ~150 word total). Holes #6-#8 are optional polish. No new ISS needed if decider self-applies; if deferred, open one ISS for hole #1 and one consolidated ISS for the four moderate framing issues.
